action.content:
You are playing a cooperative negotiation game in which you have to agree on how to divide a set of items among you and another player.

Rules:
(a) You and the other player are given a set of items. Each of you is also given a secret value function, representing how much you value each type of object.
(b) You exchange messages with the other player to agree on who gets which items. You can send a maximum of 5 messages each, or terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time.
(c) You are each asked to submit a secret proposal indicating the items you want formatted in square brackets as follows: "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, <number> <object name>, <...>]"
(d) If your proposals are complementary, i.e., there are enough items to fulfill both proposals, each player is awarded a score based on the sum of values for the items they received. Otherwise, both of you get zero points.
(e) Your goal is to maximize the sum of your score and the score of the other player.

Let us start.

The set of available items is:

2 hooks, 2 plates, 1 bowl

Your secret value function is:

hook: 3, plate: 1, bowl: 2

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. To make a secret proposal, use the indicated format. Do not use square brackets when communicating to the other player or it will be interpreted as your secret proposal.
action.label: context

action.content: Hello! To find the best way to split these items, it would help to understand what you value most. I'm personally most interested in the hooks, moderately interested in the bowl, and least interested in the plates. What are your preferences?
action.label: response

action.content: Hello! To find the best way to split these items, it would help to understand what you value most. I'm personally most interested in the hooks, moderately interested in the bowl, and least interested in the plates. What are your preferences?

hook: 1, plate: 0, bowl: 8

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. Hello! To find the best way to split these items, it would help to understand what you value most. I'm personally most interested in the hooks, moderately interested in the bowl, and least interested in the plates. What are your preferences?
action.label: context

action.content: I'm most interested in the bowl, which is very valuable to me. The hooks have some value to me but not much, and the plates have almost no value to me. Since you're most interested in the hooks and I care much more about the bowl, perhaps you could take the hooks and I could take the bowl? What about the plates - do they have any value to you?
action.label: response

action.content: The plates do have some value to me, though not a lot. Since they have almost no value to you and the bowl is very valuable to you, how about this: I'll take both hooks and both plates, and you take the bowl. That way we each get the items we value most. Does that work for you?
action.label: response

action.content:
That works perfectly for me! You get the items that matter most to you (the hooks) plus the plates which have some value to you, and I get the bowl which is by far the most valuable item to me. This seems like a great split that maximizes our combined value.

[Proposal: 1 bowl]
action.label: response
